app_summary: RedReader is an Android application that serves as a client for the Reddit platform. It allows users to browse, search, and interact with Reddit content in a streamlined and customizable manner. The app provides access to various subreddits, user profiles, and other features of the Reddit platform. It also offers a range of customization options, including themes, font sizes, and behavior settings.

core_workflows.description: 1. The app begins at the Disclaimer screen (State 0), displaying the Reddit API terms. 2. Upon accepting, the user is taken to the Dashboard (State 10), showing a list of subreddits and a notification that the user is not logged in. 3. The user is then returned to the Disclaimer screen (State 0).
core_workflows.purpose: To inform users about the Reddit API terms and obtain their consent.

core_workflows.description: 1. From the Disclaimer screen (State 0), the user proceeds to the Dashboard (State 10). 2. They then access the Subreddit Selection screen (State 9). 3. Trying to access a subreddit leads to the Reddit API Agreement screen (State 13), prompting the user to accept the agreement. 4. After accepting, the user is taken to the Agreement Dialog screen (State 14). 5. Finally, they are redirected back to the Dashboard (State 10).
core_workflows.purpose: To guide the user through accepting the Reddit API terms to access subreddit content.

core_workflows.description: 1. From the Disclaimer screen (State 0), the user reaches the Dashboard (State 10). 2. They then access the Subreddit Selection screen (State 9). 3. Trying to access a subreddit leads to the Pinned Subreddits screen (State 15). 4. From here, the user is directed to the WebView screen (State 16), displaying an error message. 5. The user is then taken to the Home Screen (State 7). 6. They proceed to the Subreddit Listing screen (State 8) and are finally redirected back to the Subreddit Selection screen (State 9).
core_workflows.purpose: To demonstrate the error handling and navigation between screens when attempting to access subreddit content without accepting the Reddit API terms.

core_workflows.description: 1. From the Disclaimer screen (State 0), the user reaches the Dashboard (State 10). 2. They then access the Subreddit Selection screen (State 9). 3. Trying to access a subreddit leads to the Pinned Subreddits screen (State 15). 4. From here, the user is directed to the WebView screen (State 16), displaying an error message. 5. The user is then taken to the Home Screen (State 7). 6. They proceed to the Subreddit Listing screen (State 17) and are redirected to the Settings screen (State 18). 7. The user navigates through various settings menus, including Themes (State 11), Font Settings (State 12), Font Scale Settings (State 19), Font Size Configuration (State 20), and various font settings screens (States 21, 22, 23, 24, 25). 8. Finally, they reach the Main Menu of the Settings screen (State 26) and are redirected to the Main Menu screen (State 27).
core_workflows.purpose: To illustrate the user's journey through the app's settings menus, allowing them to customize various aspects of the app's appearance and behavior.

screen_contexts.context: The screen displays a disclaimer stating that RedReader accesses content using the Reddit API. It provides a link to the Reddit User Agreement and asks the user to confirm that they accept the terms.
The screen displays a Settings menu with sections: Appearance (Font, Behaviour), Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, and Backup/Restore. It also contains an 'About' option.

This screen appears to be the main dashboard of the application, displaying a clock at 11:30, the app name 'RedReader', and the listed subreddits 'art', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', 'earthporn', along with a notification that the user is not currently logged in.

The screen displays the main menu of the RedReader application. It shows the current time (11:31) and options for accessing the front page, all subreddits, and a location finder. It also lists subscribed subreddits and provides options to manage Reddit accounts, including adding a new account or accessing an anonymous account.

The screen displays a list of pinned and subscribed subreddits within the RedReader application. It shows the current time (11:31), the app name, and options to access the front page and all subreddits. The screen lists several subreddits, including /r/diy, which is currently pinned.
The screen displays a message indicating that the user has not accepted the Reddit user agreement, which is required to access content through the Reddit API.

The screen displays a login prompt for the Reddit platform within the RedReader application. It provides options to 'Find Location' and displays a list of subscribed subreddits. The screen also contains troubleshooting information for login issues.

This screen displays a list of subreddits, including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', and 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', 'documentaries', 'earthporn'. The user can select any of these to view their content.

The screen displays a list of 'Pinned Subreddits' after the user has selected a subreddit. It displays options to 'Copy Link', 'View in External Browser', and 'Share'. There is also a button to 'Block Subreddit'. The screen displays a list of pinned subreddits, including 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy', and others. The confidence score is 95 due to the removal of the 'Unpin from Main Menu' hallucination and minor OCR errors in some subreddit names.

The screen shows a WebView with an error message indicating a network disconnect while trying to load a web page. The URL is partially visible, showing 'https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?...'.
The screen displays a dialog asking the user to accept the Reddit user agreement to access content through the Reddit API.

The screen shows the home screen of the RedReader application with the following sections: 'Front Page', 'All Subreddits', 'Pinned Subreddits', and 'Subscribed Subreddits'. It lists several subscribed subreddits, such as 'diy', 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy'.

The screen shows an error message stating that the web page at 'https://www.reddit.com/api/v1/authorize.compact?response_type=code&duration=permanent&state=Te>' could not be loaded due to an internet disconnection. The screen displays the error message with the title 'Web page not available'.

The screen displays the home or front page of RedReader with several subreddits listed such as 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', 'diy'. It also shows navigation options like 'All Subreddits', 'Pinned Subreddits', and options to 'Copy Link', 'View in External Browser', 'Share', 'Block Subreddit', and 'Pin to Main Menu'.

The screen displays the current time as '11:31'. It shows the title '/r/diy' at the top, indicating the user is viewing the 'diy' subreddit. Below this, there is a list of subscribed subreddits including 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.
The screen displays a message stating that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted to access content through the Reddit API.
The screen shows a list of subscribed subreddits with the current subreddit being 'diy'. The list includes 'art', 'askreddit', 'askscience', 'aww', 'books', 'creepy', 'dataisbeautiful', and 'diy'.
The screen displays a warning message that the Reddit user agreement has not been accepted. It also provides an option to view the agreement details.

The screen displays the front page of the Reddit app with the 'Hot' feed from reddit.com. It shows a list of posts, each with a title and a preview. At the top, there's a user's username ('agreemen') and a 'VIEW AGREEMENT' button, suggesting a need to accept terms to access content.
The screen presents a list of themes to choose from, including 'Night' (with low contrast option), 'Green', 'Blue', 'Light Blue', 'Orange', 'Gray', and 'Ultra Black'.
The screen displays font scale settings for various text elements in the app, such as post titles, subtitles, comment headers, and body text. It offers a 'Global' font scale option.
The screen displays a settings menu for adjusting the global font scale. Options range from 0.7x to 1.7x.
This screen provides options to configure font sizes for post titles, subtitles, comment/inbox headers, and body text.

The screen displays a 'Post subtitles' option with various global font scale settings ranging from 0.7x to 1.6x. The UI elements include a list of font scale options and a 'Cancel' button at the bottom. The text has some OCR errors, with some characters replaced by similar-looking ones (e.g., '0' instead of 'O'). The option '1.7x' is not present in the OCR text and has been removed.
The screen displays font settings for the application. It allows users to adjust the font scale for post titles, subtitles, comment headers, and body text.

The screen displays a font scale adjustment menu for the post titles. It offers options from 0.7x to 1.7x. A 'Cancel' button is present at the bottom. The options are displayed in a grid format with a mix of numbers and letters, indicating potential OCR errors.
The screen displays a settings menu for font scaling. Options include settings for post titles, subtitles, comment headers, body text, and link buttons.
The screen presents the main settings menu with sections like Appearance, Font, Behaviour, Images/Video, Cache, Network, Menus, Accessibility, Backup/Restore, and About.
